title: Java Lambda Expressions
content:
### Lambda Expressions

Lambda expressions provide a concise way to implement functional interfaces (interfaces with a single abstract method).

---

### Lambda Syntax

```java
(parameter1, parameter2) -> { body }
(parameter) -> expression
() -> expression
```

---

### Examples

| Lambda | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `() -> 42` | No args, returns 42 |
| `(x) -> x * 2` | One arg, returns doubled |
| `(a, b) -> a + b` | Two args, returns sum |
| `s -> s.length()` | One arg, returns length |
| `(x, y) -> { return x + y; }` | Block body with return |

---

### Functional Interfaces

| Interface | Method | Usage |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `Predicate<T>` | `boolean test(T)` | Filtering |
| `Function<T,R>` | `R apply(T)` | Transformation |
| `Consumer<T>` | `void accept(T)` | Side effects |
| `Supplier<T>` | `T get()` | Lazy generation |
| `UnaryOperator<T>` | `T apply(T)` | Same type transform |
| `BinaryOperator<T>` | `T apply(T,T)` | Same type combine |

---

### Method References

| Type | Syntax | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Static method | `Class::staticMethod` | `Math::max` |
| Instance method | `object::instanceMethod` | `list::add` |
| Constructor | `Class::new` | `ArrayList::new` |

> **Key Point:** Lambdas can only be used with functional interfaces. The target type is inferred from context. Use method references for cleaner code when the lambda just calls an existing method.

quiz.question: Which annotation marks a functional interface?
